definitions.technical: The risk that an entity cannot convert its assets to cash quickly enough to meet its obligations. The comment letter distinguishes two subtypes: market-side liquidity risk (inability to sell an asset at par) and receivership-side liquidity risk (inability to access an asset frozen in FDIC proceedings).
definitions.lay: The risk that a company cannot turn its assets into cash fast enough to pay what it owes. For stablecoin issuers, this comes in two forms: difficulty selling assets at full price in bad markets, and inability to access assets trapped at a failed bank.
definitions.parenthetical: (the risk of being unable to convert assets to cash quickly enough to meet obligations, including both market and receivership subtypes)
definitions.legal_significance: The comment letter's central argument is that the proposed rule addresses market-side liquidity risk through reserve composition requirements but fails to address receivership-side liquidity risk. This distinction is critical because the SVB episode involved the latter, not the former.
